title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers publisher confirms, windows, failure ambiguity, and republish into a new confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.
Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Confirm Channel Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 1569 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Confirm Channel Contract.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Publisher Ledger Cleanup structural check is only one gate. confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Publisher Ledger Cleanup, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Publisher Ledger Cleanup precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Publisher Ledger Cleanup, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab messaging team handles case 3811 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Publisher Ledger Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The client library automatically retains a durable application ledger across reconnection.

lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 5430 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Publisher Ledger Cleanup without its stated preconditions, omit the confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Publisher Ledger Cleanup for confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Publisher Ledger Cleanup condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Publisher Ledger Cleanup output and hide the violated confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Publisher Ledger Cleanup.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Publisher Ledger Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Publisher Ledger Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab messaging team handles case 7049 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.
